doctor_turn: Hello, I’m Dr. Lee. What brought you in today?
patient_turn: Hi, um, I’m here because I’ve been having this pain in my right knee for a few weeks now. It started out just after I was active, but now it hurts even when I’m just walking or, you know, going upstairs. It’s kinda worrying me…

doctor_turn: When did you first notice the pain in your knee?
patient_turn: I think it was, um, a few weeks ago? At first, it was just after I played soccer or basketball, but then it started hurting more, like, even when I’m not doing anything. It’s been getting worse, I guess…

doctor_turn: Can you point to where exactly the pain is in your knee?
patient_turn: Yeah, it’s, um, right below the kneecap, like in the front of my knee. It’s kinda achy when I’m resting, but it gets sharp if I touch it directly. It’s really uncomfortable…

doctor_turn: Does the pain ever spread to other areas, or is it just in that spot?
patient_turn: Well, it’s mostly just in that spot below my kneecap, but, you know, I was thinking about how I’ve been running a lot lately, and I also play basketball, which can be tough on the knees. And, oh! I remember last summer I twisted my ankle during a game, but that was a while ago. So, yeah, just that spot really.

doctor_turn: How would you describe the pain? Is it sharp, dull, or something else?
patient_turn: Um, it’s mostly achy when I’m resting, but it gets really sharp if I touch it directly. I’d say it’s like a 6 or 7 out of 10 in severity, which is, you know, pretty uncomfortable. It’s just frustrating because I want to keep playing sports…

doctor_turn: Does the pain stay in one spot, or does it spread to other areas?
patient_turn: It pretty much stays in that one spot below my kneecap. I haven’t noticed it spreading anywhere else, which is, um, a relief, I guess? But it’s still really uncomfortable, and I just want it to get better…

doctor_turn: How would you describe the pain? Is it sharp, dull, achy, or something else?
patient_turn: Oh, it’s definitely sharp, but I think it’s also kind of dull sometimes, too. I mean, I could say it’s mostly achy, but then again, I don’t really feel it that much unless I’m doing something, like running or jumping. So, I guess it’s not really sharp at all, actually.

doctor_turn: Have you had any previous injuries or medical conditions related to your knee or any other part of your body?
patient_turn: Um, not really. I haven’t had any previous injuries or medical conditions, like, related to my knee or anything else. I did twist my ankle last summer during a game, but that was a while ago and it’s been fine since then. So, yeah, nothing else really…

doctor_turn: Are you currently taking any medications on a regular basis?
patient_turn: No, I’m not taking any medications regularly. I don’t have any allergies or anything like that, so, um, I guess I’m just trying to manage the pain with, you know, ice and stuff for now.

doctor_turn: Do you have any known allergies to medications or anything else?
patient_turn: Um, no, I don’t have any known allergies to medications or anything else. I’ve never had any issues with that, so, yeah, I’m all good there.

doctor_turn: Have you experienced any recent illnesses or fevers?
patient_turn: No, I haven’t had any recent illnesses or fevers. I’ve been feeling fine otherwise, just dealing with this knee pain, you know? It’s kinda frustrating…

doctor_turn: Have you noticed any changes in your bowel or urinary habits recently?
patient_turn: Um, no, I haven’t noticed any changes in my bowel or urinary habits. Everything seems normal there, it’s just this knee pain that’s really bothering me. I’m kinda worried about it, to be honest…

doctor_turn: Does anyone in your family have a history of knee problems or injuries?
patient_turn: No, um, my family doesn’t have a history of knee problems or injuries. I think everyone’s been pretty healthy, so it’s just me dealing with this right now. It’s a bit frustrating, you know?

doctor_turn: Who do you live with at home?
patient_turn: I live with both my parents and my younger sister. It’s, um, nice having them around, but I feel kinda bad that I can’t be as active with them because of my knee pain. I just want to get back to normal…

doctor_turn: Have you felt any tightness or discomfort in your thigh muscles recently?
patient_turn: Yeah, I’ve noticed some tightness in my thigh muscles. It’s been, um, a bit uncomfortable, especially when I’m trying to run or jump. I’m not totally sure if that’s related to my knee pain, but it’s definitely something I’ve felt…

doctor_turn: Would you like to talk about any imaging options for your knee pain?
patient_turn: Um, I guess I’m open to talking about imaging options if it helps figure out what’s going on. I mean, I hope it’s nothing serious, but if my symptoms don’t improve, then maybe it would be a good idea to look into it. I just really want to get back to playing sports…
